---
title: "Verification: Remove Replicate-Based RNG Seeding"
date: 2026-04-24
categories: [verification]
status: in-progress
branch: "refactor/rep-seed-strat"
pr: 0
---

This notebook walks through every verification step for `refactor/rep-seed-strat`, which removes the `rep`-as-RNG-seed pattern from `bin/create_causal_vars.py` and `bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R`, and drops the `sim_seed int32` field from the trait metadata schema.

Work through the steps in order. Steps 1–4 require only the cloned repo and no pipeline data; Steps 5–6 require a completed pipeline run.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Summary Checklist

Fill in the **Result** column as you work through each step.

| \# | Step | Environment | Pass criterion | Result |
|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|
| 0 | `NXF_VER=24.10.5` pinned | local + HPC | `nextflow -version` shows 24.10.x | PASS |
| 1a | stub-run `test` | local | exits 0, no wiring errors | PASS |
| 1b | stub-run `test,docker` | local | exits 0 | PASS |
| 1c | stub-run `test_variable` | local | exits 0 | PASS |
| 1d | stub-run `test_cv_pool` | local | exits 0 | PASS |
| 1e | stub-run `test_three_species` | local | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 2a | stub-run `test_hpc,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 2b | stub-run `test_hpc_variable,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 2c | stub-run `test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 2d | stub-run `test_hpc_three_species,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 3a | `Rscript tests/run_tests.R` | local | all pass/skip | \[ \] |
| 3b | schema spot-check | local R session | 12 fields, no `sim_seed` | PASS |
| 3c | round-trip write/read | local R session | read-back succeeds, no `sim_seed` | PASS |
| 4a | Python script unseeded | local | `diff` exits non-zero | PASS |
| 4b | R script unseeded | local | `diff` exits non-zero | \[ \] |
| 4c | Batch-independence | local | `diff` exits non-zero | \[ \] |
| 5a | full run `test` + integration tests | local | all tests pass | PASS |
| 5b | full run `test_variable` + tests | local | all tests pass | PASS |
| 5c | full run `test_cv_pool` + tests | local | all tests pass | \[ \] |
| 5d | trait parquet schema check | local R | 12-col, no `sim_seed` | \[ \] |
| 6a | HPC run `test_hpc,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0, assessment TSV present | \[ \] |
| 6b | HPC run `test_hpc_variable,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 6c | HPC run `test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |
| 6d | HPC run `test_hpc_three_species,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | \[ \] |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 0. Nextflow version pinning

The pipeline requires Nextflow `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0`. Nextflow 25.x introduced breaking changes to the `nextflow.preview.output` publish-block API used here. Pin the exact version with the `NXF_VER` environment variable **before every run** on both local and HPC environments.

``` bash
# Pin to the latest stable 24.10.x release.
# Check https://github.com/nextflow-io/nextflow/releases for the current 24.10.x patch.
export NXF_VER=24.10.5

# Verify the active version
nextflow -version
# Expected: nextflow version 24.10.5.XXXX
```

::: callout-warning
If `nextflow -version` reports 25.x, all stub-runs and full runs below will emit publish-block warnings. These are **pre-existing** on machines with system Nextflow ≥ 25.x and are unrelated to this refactor — but set `NXF_VER=24.10.5` anyway to avoid false alarm output cluttering the verification results.
:::

Add to your shell profile so it persists across sessions on the local machine:

``` bash
echo 'export NXF_VER=24.10.5' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc
```

On Rockfish, set `NXF_VER` in the job submission script or your `~/.bashrc` before loading the Nextflow module:

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
module load nextflow/24.10.5   # or whichever module name your cluster uses
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Pipeline wiring — stub-runs (local)

Stub-runs skip all computation and confirm only that Nextflow can resolve every `input:` / `output:` declaration. Run one per relevant local profile. None of these require installed tools, VCFs, or containers — they complete in seconds.

All commands assume you are in the project root.

### 1a. Fixed architecture (`test`)

The primary local test profile: 1 population, 5 nQTL, 0.8 h², 1 replicate.

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0; all `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA` task stubs complete without `Missing output file` or `No such variable` errors.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 1b. Fixed architecture with Docker (`test,docker`)

Confirms the Docker config and module wiring together.

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test,docker -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** same as 1a.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 1c. Variable architecture (`test_variable`)

Exercises the broader parameter grid: multiple nQTL × h² × 2 replicates.

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_variable -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0 for all combinations in the cartesian product.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 1d. Non-marker CV pool (`test_cv_pool`)

Exercises the code path where the causal-variant pool (`cv_maf=0.01`) is broader than the marker set (`ms_maf=0.05`).

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_cv_pool -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0; `PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL` stubs complete.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 1e. Three-species path (`test_three_species`)

Exercises the mixed-species pipeline path without requiring the `c_briggsae` / `c_tropicalis` test VCFs (stub-run skips bcftools extraction).

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_three_species -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0 across all 3 species × population groups.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Pipeline wiring — stub-runs (HPC)

Run on Rockfish. These confirm the Singularity + SLURM wiring works with the full VCF paths. Requires `hpc_strains.txt` (generate with `bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh` if absent).

### 2a. HPC fixed architecture (`test_hpc,rockfish`)

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc,rockfish -stub-run
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 2b. HPC variable architecture (`test_hpc_variable,rockfish`)

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish -stub-run
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 2c. HPC non-marker CV pool (`test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish`)

Note: `--legacy_assess` is omitted — the legacy R path is incompatible with non-marker causal variants.

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish -stub-run
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 2d. HPC three-species (`test_hpc_three_species,rockfish`)

Requires all three HPC VCF paths in `hpc_strains_three_species.txt`.

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria for all HPC stub-runs:** exits 0; no SLURM submission errors (stub-run does not submit real jobs but does resolve the `clusterOptions` and container directives).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. R schema and unit tests

Verifies that `trait_metadata_schema()` now has 12 fields (no `sim_seed`), and that `write_trait_metadata()` compiles and runs without the removed parameter.

### 3a. Full test suite

``` bash
cd /path/to/refactor-rep-seed-strat

Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

**Pass criteria:**

-   All tests pass or skip cleanly (integration tests auto-skip when `TEST_DB_DIR` is unset).
-   No `unused argument (sim_seed = ...)` errors.
-   `test-db_structure.R` "trait metadata parquets have correct schema" test: if integration data is present, asserts 12 expected columns and no `sim_seed` key.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 3b. Schema spot-check (R session, no pipeline data required)

Open an R session in the project root, or run as a script:

``` r
# Source only database.R (it has no external pipeline deps)
source("R/utils.R")
source("R/io.R")
source("R/database.R")

schema <- trait_metadata_schema()

cat("Field count:", length(schema$names), "\n")
cat("Fields:\n  ", paste(schema$names, collapse = "\n   "), "\n")

stopifnot(
  !"sim_seed" %in% schema$names,
  length(schema$names) == 12L
)
cat("PASS: 12-column schema, sim_seed absent\n")
```

Expected output:

```         
Field count: 12
Fields:
   trait_id
   trait_hash_string
   marker_set_id
   nqtl
   rep
   h2
   maf
   effect
   population
   cv_maf_effective
   cv_ld
   created_at
PASS: 12-column schema, sim_seed absent
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 3c. Round-trip write/read without `sim_seed`

Confirms `write_trait_metadata()` writes and reads back cleanly under the new signature.

``` r
source("R/utils.R")
source("R/io.R")
source("R/database.R")

tmp <- tempdir()
init_database(tmp)

# Build a minimal trait_id for the round-trip
ids <- build_ids_from_params(list(
  population = "testpop", maf = 0.05, species = "c_elegans",
  vcf_release_id = "test", ms_ld = 0.8,
  nqtl = 5L, effect = "gamma", rep = 1L, h2 = 0.8,
  cv_maf_effective = 0.05, cv_ld = 0.8
))
trait_id <- ids$trait_id$hash

write_trait_metadata(
  trait_id          = trait_id,
  trait_hash_string = ids$trait_id$hash_string,
  marker_set_id     = ids$ms_id$hash,
  nqtl             = 5L,
  rep              = 1L,
  h2               = 0.8,
  maf              = 0.05,
  effect           = "gamma",
  population       = "testpop",
  cv_maf_effective = 0.05,
  cv_ld            = 0.8,
  base_dir         = tmp
)

result <- arrow::read_parquet(get_trait_metadata_path(trait_id, tmp))
stopifnot(
  !"sim_seed" %in% names(result),
  result$rep == 1L,
  result$nqtl == 5L
)
cat("PASS: round-trip write/read succeeded without sim_seed\n")
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Unseeded script behavior

Runs each stochastic script twice with identical arguments and confirms the outputs differ — proving that OS-entropy seeding produces independent draws rather than a deterministic rep-seeded sequence.

Under the old design, both runs would produce identical files (same seed = `rep` value). A non-zero `diff` exit code is the **expected** post-refactor result.

### 4a. Python: `create_causal_vars.py`

Requires a working Python environment with `numpy` and `pandas` (or run inside the Docker container). The test VCF's `.bim` file is used as the marker pool.

``` bash
cd /tmp   # work outside the repo to keep outputs separate

BIM=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/fixtures/test.bim
SCRIPT=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/bin/create_causal_vars.py

# Run 1: nqtl=5, effect=gamma, rep=1
python3 "$SCRIPT" "$BIM" 5 gamma 1
mv causal_vars.txt causal_vars_run1.txt

# Run 2: identical arguments
python3 "$SCRIPT" "$BIM" 5 gamma 1
mv causal_vars.txt causal_vars_run2.txt

diff causal_vars_run1.txt causal_vars_run2.txt
echo "diff exit code: $? (expected: non-zero = files differ = PASS)"
```

To find the correct `.bim` path after a local test run:

``` bash
find Analysis_Results-*/work -name "CV_TO_SIMS.bim" | head -1
```

**Pass criteria:** `diff` exits non-zero; the two output files contain different variant selections and/or effect sizes.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 4b. R: `Create_Causal_QTLs.R`

``` bash
cd /tmp

BIM=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/fixtures/test.bim
SCRIPT=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R

# Run 1: nqtl=5, effect=gamma, rep=1
Rscript --vanilla "$SCRIPT" "$BIM" 5 gamma 1
mv causal.variants.sim.5.txt causal_variants_run1.txt

# Run 2: identical arguments
Rscript --vanilla "$SCRIPT" "$BIM" 5 gamma 1
mv causal.variants.sim.5.txt causal_variants_run2.txt

diff causal_variants_run1.txt causal_variants_run2.txt
echo "diff exit code: $? (expected: non-zero = files differ = PASS)"
```

**Pass criteria:** `diff` exits non-zero.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Integration tests — local pipeline run

Runs the full pipeline locally and validates the resulting database. Requires Docker and the test VCF (`data/test/` directory). The `collect_test_data.sh` helper both runs the pipeline and collects its output into the `tests/integration_data/` directories expected by the test suite.

### 5a. Fixed architecture

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test
```

Then run the integration test suite with the collected data:

``` bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

**Pass criteria:** all integration tests pass; `test-db_structure.R` "trait metadata parquets have correct schema" reports no missing columns and no `sim_seed` assertion failures.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 5b. Variable architecture

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
Completed at: 24-Apr-2026 12:01:53
Duration    : 2h 17m 48s
CPU hours   : 14.8
Succeeded   : 2'909


Collecting outputs from: /Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/Analysis_Results-test_variable
Collected 2621 cross-validation files to: /Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/integration_data/test_variable/work

Test data collected to: /Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/integration_data/test_variable

Run integration tests with:

  TEST_DB_DIR=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
  TEST_WORK_DIR=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/integration_data/test_variable/work \
  TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
  TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/refactor-rep-seed-strat/tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
  Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```
:::

``` bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=256 \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
assessment_cross_validation: ..........................
cross_validation: ................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
cv_pool: SSSSSSSS
db_structure: ......................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................S...

══ Skipped ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. traits/causal_genotypes/ directory exists and contains parquet files (test-cv_pool.R:73:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

2. causal genotype parquets have correct schema (test-cv_pool.R:87:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

3. non-marker causal variants appear as FN rows with NA log10p in assessment (test-cv_pool.R:109:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

4. non-marker FN rows have significant=NA (not FALSE) in assessment (test-cv_pool.R:126:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

5. Simulated.QTL.VarExp is populated for non-marker FN rows (test-cv_pool.R:141:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

6. non-marker causal variants appear in causal_variants/ but not in markers DB view (test-cv_pool.R:157:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

7. designate_qtl() classifies non-marker rows correctly by Detected status (test-cv_pool.R:188:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

8. calculate_simrep_performance() runs correctly on cv_pool assessment data (test-cv_pool.R:223:3) - Reason: TEST_CV_POOL=true not set — skipping cv_pool integration tests

9. all expected populations appear in mappings_metadata and are queryable (test-db_structure.R:490:5) - Reason: TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS not set — skipping multi-population assertions

══ DONE ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Warning message:
Connection is garbage-collected, use dbDisconnect() to avoid this. 
```
:::

### 5c. Non-marker CV pool (no legacy assess)

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_cv_pool --no-legacy
```

``` bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/work \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 5d. Spot-check trait parquet schema from any local run

``` r
library(arrow)

db_dir <- "tests/integration_data/test/db"   # adjust to whichever profile you ran

trait_files <- list.files(
  file.path(db_dir, "traits"),
  pattern = "^[0-9a-f]{20}\\.parquet$",
  full.names = TRUE
)
stopifnot(length(trait_files) > 0)

schema <- read_parquet(trait_files[1], as_data_frame = FALSE)$schema
cat("Columns:", paste(names(schema), collapse = ", "), "\n")

stopifnot(!"sim_seed" %in% names(schema))
stopifnot(length(names(schema)) == 12L)
cat("PASS: 12-column schema, no sim_seed\n")
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Integration tests — HPC pipeline run (Rockfish)

Run on Rockfish after the local checks pass. These use full-scale VCFs and SLURM submission.

### 6a. Fixed architecture

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc
# collect_test_data.sh auto-appends ",rockfish" for HPC profiles
```

Or manually:

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc,rockfish --legacy_assess
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 6b. Variable architecture

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish --legacy_assess
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 6c. Non-marker CV pool

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish
# --legacy_assess intentionally omitted: incompatible with non-marker CVs
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::

### 6d. Three-species

``` bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish --legacy_assess
```

**Pass criteria for all HPC runs:** pipeline exits 0; `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` present in the output directory; `traits/` parquet files have 12-column schema (spot-check with the R snippet from Step 5d, pointing `db_dir` at the HPC run's `db/` output).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Output"}
``` text
[paste output here]
```
:::
